text: CFTC Chair Michael Selig, currently the agency's only sitting commissioner, effectively holds key regulatory decision-making power over crypto assets, prediction markets and derivatives markets. text: An Overview of Modern AI Robotics from First Principles Modern AI robotics has shifted toward Vision-Language-Action (VLA) architectures, which couple a slow, large backbone model for environmental understanding with a fast, specialized action expert that predicts continuous chunks of motor commands under strict inference deadlines To overcome the severe real-world data bottleneck, the field relies on scalable solutions like human egocentric video data, simulated environments generated by world models, and self-improvement frameworks like RECAP that combine human coaching with autonomous practice

text: An Investor Relations Dashboard Story: How PayPal's PYUSD vault became Sparks #1 gross revenue line, and why DefiLlama is the only place you can see it Spark rotated much of its allocation to PYUSD, now the protocol's largest revenue venue. This is an offchain, CeFi-adjacent yield source flowing into a DeFi protocol's P&L, and it's only visible because of this income statement feature in Spark's IR Dashboard on DefiLlama. Back in Dec 2025, PayPal launched the spPYUSD savings vault on Spark. Within the first 24hr, $200 million flowed in. By Q1 2026, the "PayPal Yields" line in Spark's income statement grew to $13.69M, with the protocol retaining $2.18M (a 15.9% margin). Explore Spark's IR Dashboard and see for yourself: > Full P&L waterfall > Net revenue by product > SLL capital allocation > Treasury health vs buyback threshold > SPK buyback tracker > OpEx projections

text: Citigroup Rolls Out Tokenized Shares of Private Companies According to WSJ, Citigroup is establishing a framework enabling its wealthy and institutional clients to trade shares of private, pre-IPO companies leveraging blockchain technology—a venture it envisions being adopted by peer firms across Wall Street. The banking giant is currently in discussions with some of the largest private enterprises to secure their involvement. The platform operates via tokenized depositary receipts—securities that represent fractional ownership in foreign corporate shares—which have been authorized, minted, and structured on-chain, with Citigroup serving as the foundational issuer and custodian. — link

text: Guide for people making a career in the age of AI True security lies in traits machines cannot replicate: building genuine interpersonal relationships, gaining "tacit knowledge" through hands-on experiences out in the physical world, and preserving your independent thinking rather than letting AI replace your cognitive heavy lifting The traditional 50-year stable career path is over, meaning long-term prestige matters less than the ability to constantly reinvent yourself Success requires being comfortable with low-status entry points, actively troubleshooting complex real-world problems where AI agents get stuck, and maintaining a stubborn belief that you can find a workaround to unblock outcomes Most importantly, just be genuine yourself

text: Exciting asymmetric trade ideas - Part 1 1. World Action Models (WAM) - a new vertical in robotics which is a successor of VLA's/VLM's. Data collection through teleoperation is largely inefficient, humanized sensors are massively growing and companies adjacent to them. Nvidia defines them as models that predict future world states and actions. DreamZero see's WAMs as a way to learn physical dynamics and generalize to unseen motions where standard VLAs can struggle. DreamDojo pushes the same direction, pretraining a robot world model on 44k hours of egocentric human video before small robot specific post training. Trade expression: The better longs are the humanized sensor and data layer like smart glasses, body and wrist capture, machine vision components, labeling/filtering infrastructure and world model/synthetic data tooling. We've seen the adoption of smart glasses already with Meta/Ray ban glasses constantly getting viral clips. Essentially you're long capture/data infrastructure vs. robot OEMs. Counterpoints: Abundant human video is still not the same as robot supervision. Robot demonstrations are expensive and embodiment centred, since human videos typically lack aligned action labels and proprioceptive context. Meaning teleoperation probably gets compressed, not removed. WAM's should reduce its relative important, but shorter term they still need alignment layers, embodiment bridges and robot specific post training. 2. EgoScale and Scaling Laws - EgoScale trained a VLA on 20k hours of action labeled egocentric human video, found a log linear scaling law between human data scale and validation loss, showed that validation loss strongly tracks downstream robot performance and reported a 54% average robot success improvement over no pretraining on a 22 DoF dexterous hand. This is the first serious evidence of predictable dexterity scaling. Trade expression: Things like data acquisition, data refinement and representation enrichment. AoE proposes always on egocentric collection using smartphones plus cloud edge labelling, specifically to lower collection cost and expand scene coverage. ActiveMimic argues that a major missing signal in human video is active perception, camera motion that existing pipelines treat as noise and shows that modelling it can close majority of the gap to robot data pretraining. So the investable angle is not just "more hours" but cheaper capture + better auxiliary signals such as wrist views, head motion, gaze/camera motion and lightweight mid training for alignment. Nvidia is looking to combine WAMs and EgoScale scaling laws closely. Counterpoints: It's still early days and it's important to not over extrapolate a few scaling papers into a confident moat. I haven't gone deep enough down the rabbit hole yet but EgoScale still uses a two stage recipe with human pretraining plus aligned human robot mid training. Activemimic's whole contribution is that egocentric pretraining had been underperforming robot data pretraining because critical signals were missing. Meaning the "durable" moat may end up being data cleaning, synchronization and representation engineering, not ownership of raw hours alone.

text: Bitcoin Spot ETFs See $214 Million Outflow as Ether ETFs Lose $35.6 Million According to SoSoValue data, on June 10 (Eastern Time), Bitcoin spot ETFs recorded a total net outflow of USD 214 million, with Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF BTC seeing the largest single-day net inflow of USD 17.5167 million. Ethereum spot ETFs recorded a total net outflow of USD 35.5931 million, while BlackRock's Staked ETH ETF ETHB saw the largest single-day net inflow at USD 1.6751 million. — link

text: The Race to Trade Everything The prediction market sector is shifting toward a "trade everything from one account" model Prediction giants like Kalshi and Polymarket are expanding into perpetual futures, while the premier onchain perpetuals exchange, Hyperliquid, has entered the event-trading arena via its new HIP-4 upgrade

text: Policy on the AI Exponential Anthropic CEO argues that the rapid, exponential advancement of frontier AI models introduces immediate strategic risks to national security, labor markets, and civil liberties that slow-moving traditional legislative bodies can't handle To bridge this gap, he proposes binding, internationally coordinated regulations modeled after aviation safety, alongside proactive macroeconomic and geopolitical frameworks designed to secure democratic leadership and distribute AI's massive potential benefits equitably
author: admin#1742094092

text: Citadel Securities on Tokenomics (not the tokenomics you wanted to see) Citadel argues AI adoption is shifting from what models can do to what their inputs cost, with the falling Silicon Data LLM Expenditure Index signaling users substituting toward cheaper, more efficient models where frontier capability isn't required They see a bifurcation forming - inference-heavy frontier work concentrating among a few firms with the balance sheets to absorb the cost, while broader productivity gains come from embedding AI as a cheaper complement to human labor rather than autonomous agents "running everything everywhere"

text: Hermes: The Moat Above The Model Open and closed models have converged on benchmarks. Inference cost collapsed from $30/1M tokens to ~$0.05. So the model no longer differentiates. What matters is the layer above it: memory, skills, context management, error recovery. Nous open-sourced a runtime like Claude Code. Memory and skills stay local and user-owned. It went from 4th to 1st on OpenRouter in eight weeks. It now routes ~3.2T tokens/week. The claimed loop: sessions generate behavioral data, that data improves post-training, better models attract more users. Revenue comes from tiered Nous Portal access

text: Highlight Clip Michael Saylor: $400 Billion Tech IPOs Drive Short-Term Bitcoin Decline On June 5, 2026, Michael Saylor stated during a TradePMR official livestream that massive capital raisings totaling approximately $400 billion by tech giants such as OpenAI, Google, and SpaceX, driven by AI infrastructure demands, have triggered a global capital rotation. He believes that as Wall Street investment banks actively market these epic IPOs, market participants are selling off various stable assets, including Bitcoin, to secure cash for these new issues. This has created a massive capital vacuum effect, causing Bitcoin to drop from $82,000 to the $62,000–$63,000 range over 15 days. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: CryptoQuant Sees Bitcoin's Potential Bottom Near $53,600 CryptoQuant said Bitcoin's potential bottom may be near $53,600, its current realized price, though research head Julio Moreno stressed this is only a "valuation bottom candidate," not a confirmed cycle bottom. The firm said Bitcoin demand remains deeply unfavorable, with total demand falling by 652,000 BTC last week, 30-day ETF demand growth dropping to -74,000 BTC, and realized holder losses over the past 30 days still below historic capitulation levels. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: On-Chain Analysis Revives Claims Hoskinson Sold 1.5 Billion ADA in 2021 Rally NFT creator Masato Alexander published on-chain analysis claiming Cardano co-founder Charles Hoskinson may have sold about 1.5 billion ADA during the 2021 bull market, when ADA hit its $3.09 all-time high. The analysis traced large ADA flows, including a 925 million ADA transfer and several 20 million ADA payments, to funding sources linked to IOG-related stake pools, though it does not prove the tokens were sold. The Cardano Foundation said it had no insight into the IOG transactions referenced in the X thread, but had no reason to assume anything other than professional conduct and good faith by other founding entities, including Hoskinson. — link
author: admin#1534592031
